message:
refactor(core): replace tokio::test with CompatRuntime in wait module tests

Migrate all async tests in the wait module from #[tokio::test] to a
run_async_test() helper that uses the crate's own CompatRuntime
(RuntimeBuilder::current_thread()), aligning the test infrastructure
with the project's runtime abstraction layer.

Why this change:
- The wait module's production code uses CompatRuntime, not tokio
  directly. Tests should exercise the same runtime path to catch
  runtime-specific behavioral differences (timer resolution,
  task scheduling, waker semantics)
- Eliminates a direct tokio dev-dependency from this module's test
  surface, reducing coupling to a specific async runtime
- All 7 async tests converted: wait_for_value_timeout_includes_debug_info,
  wait_for_value_succeeds_immediately, wait_for_predicate_succeeds_after_n_attempts,
  wait_for_predicate_timeout_reports_last_state, wait_for_cancellation_after_retries,
  wait_for_max_retries_exceeded, backoff_jitter_stays_in_bounds

Each test now wraps its async body in run_async_test(async { ... })
using a current-thread CompatRuntime, preserving identical test logic
and assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
